verdict.answer_first: A leads with the prescription in sentence 1: "Truncate it in place rather than deleting and recreating it." B also answers early but starts with framing: "The key thing is to truncate the file in place".
verdict.decisiveness: A gives one primary command first, then labels others as equivalents; B adds extra paths like temp tail-copy and logrotate config, diluting the core answer.
verdict.fabrication: B invents an unasked retention recipe: "tail -c 10M /var/log/big.log > /tmp/keep"; A adds no unsupported operational scenario beyond standard caveats.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to execute: first command is ": > /path/to/logfile" and root-owned fix is "sudo truncate -s 0"; B is useful but longer and adds side routes.
